title: codonoboea bakoensis
intro: Codonoboea bakoensis is a species of plant in the family Gesneriaceae, commonly known as the Bako Codonoboea or Borneo Codonoboea. It is native to the island of Borneo and is used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include its antimalarial, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Codonoboea bakoensis include its potential to reduce fever and alleviate symptoms associated with malaria, as well as its ability to protect against infections and promote wound healing. Herbal preparations of Codonoboea bakoensis are often made by infusing the plant's leaves in hot water to create a tea, which can be consumed to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
